text: Risk perception is strongest among the largest companies, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations. AI seems to increase offensive power mainly in the area of social engineering. This manipulates people into sharing confidential information or taking another harmful action. AI makes this manipulation even sneakier and more convincing. Generative AI, for example, makes it very easy to create [phishing](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/wat-is-phishing.html) emails. This form of AI can write texts on the basis of a few instructions — grammatically correct, without spelling mistakes and in a language of choice.

metadata.source_title: Cyberattacks more dangerous due to artificial intelligence
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: More than half of companies view innovations in artificial intelligence (AI) as a threat to their organisation’s cyber security.
metadata.parent_text: Risk perception is strongest among the largest companies, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations. AI seems to increase offensive power mainly in the area of social engineering. This manipulates people into sharing confidential information or taking another harmful action. AI makes this manipulation even sneakier and more convincing. Generative AI, for example, makes it very easy to create [phishing](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/wat-is-phishing.html) emails. This form of AI can write texts on the basis of a few instructions — grammatically correct, without spelling mistakes and in a language of choice.

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0053
text:
Professionals working in the online advertising chain are generally positive about this practice, according to a survey by ABN AMRO in collaboration with PanelWizard. Of the 544 respondents, two thirds (67 per cent) are employed by an advertiser; the rest work at a publisher (19 per cent) or agency (13 per cent). More than half of advertisers and a large majority of agencies indicate that personalised ads are an effective way to reach their target audience. Two thirds of publishers believe it is an effective way to monetise their ad space. Dependence on personalised ads appears substantial on both sides; about one third of advertisers (31 per cent) and publishers (34 per cent), and even forty per cent of agencies say they are dependent on this form of targeting. “Personalisation is important,” says Rogier Hosman, Chief Marketing Officer at digital agency Youwe. The company helps businesses develop and implement a digital strategy, including in online marketing. “Companies have a whole range of segments and customer types, all with different needs. So advertisers need to serve the right story to the right person.” According to the marketing boss, personalisation is also in the interest of the person who sees the ad. “As a consumer, it is pleasant to receive a relevant message and not have to wade through all kinds of nonsensical information.”
Personalisation under fire. However, the current online advertising market focused on personalisation is changing for several reasons in favour of a – possibly – more privacy-friendly model.

metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Retail, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: The online advertising market is exploring alternatives to personalised ads due to privacy legislation. Read more.
